document.title: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector, File No. A-TCF-5-056 Rev.0, dated MAY/27/2020.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents the usability engineering process, application specification, hazard identification, user interface requirements, usability verification, validation references, and residual risk conclusion for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
keyPoints: The document applies to ADD, described as an Abyz Digital Detector and Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector.
The intended use is digital radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding mammography.
The intended user profile identifies radiologists and professional hospital use.
Referenced standards include EN/IEC 60601-1-6, EN/IEC 62366, and EN/ISO 14971.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are recorded as Pass with no issues.
Usability validation is referenced to Usability Validation Report Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040.

entities.name: R&D team
entities.evidence: Prepared R&D team Jung, Woosuk MAY/27/2020

entities.name: QA representative
entities.evidence: Approval QA representative Hur Namkyu MAY/27/2020

concepts.name: Usability Engineering Process
concepts.evidence: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e. NORMAL USE.

concepts.name: Application Specification
concepts.evidence: Application specification - Description, intended use - Medical purpose - Intended PATIENT population - Intended part of the body or type of tissue applied to or interacted with - Intended user profile - Intended conditions of use - Operating principle

concepts.name: Intended Medical Indication
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Frequently Used Functions
concepts.evidence: Shall determine the frequently used functions that involve user interaction with the medical device.

concepts.name: Hazards and Hazardous Situations Related to Usability
concepts.evidence: Shall identify known or foreseeable HAZARDS (part of a RISK ANALYSIS) related to usability according to ISO 14971:2012, 4.3.

concepts.name: Primary Operating Functions
concepts.evidence: Function that involves USER interaction that is either frequently used or related to the SAFETY of the MEDICAL DEVICE.

concepts.name: Usability Specification
concepts.evidence: Documentation defining the USER INTERFACE requirements related to USABILITY.

concepts.name: Usability Verification
concepts.evidence: Shall verify the implementation of the medical device user interface design against the requirements of the usability specification.

concepts.name: Usability Validation
concepts.evidence: Shall validate usability of the medical device according to the usability validation plan.

concepts.name: User Interface Design and Implementation
concepts.pageTarget: ADD user interface design and implementation
concepts.evidence: We design the User Interface including the following requirements.

evidence.claim: The document is File No. A-TCF-5-056, Rev. No. 0, with revision date MAY/27/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-TCF-5-056 Rev. No 0 Rev. Date MAY/27/2020

evidence.claim: The report number is A-TCF-5-056 Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-056 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The revision history records Rev. 0 as establishment on MAY/27/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 MAY/27/2020 Establishment

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz

evidence.claim: The product name is Abyz Digital Detector and the model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: The ADD is indicated for digital imaging for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes adults and children and excludes pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Age: Adult and Children ... Patient group/state: except pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended anatomy includes all parts such as head, chest, spine, abdomen, hand, feet, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Using part: All parts (e.g. Head, Chest, Spine, Abdomen, Hand, feet and internal organs)

evidence.claim: The intended user experience is radiologist.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: The device is intended for professional hospital use.
evidence.evidenceText: Hospital, intended for professional use

evidence.claim: Operating conditions are 5℃ to 40℃, 30 to 75% relative humidity non-condensing, and 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation condition - Temperature: 5℃ to 40℃ - Humidity: 30 to 75% relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: Storage conditions are -10℃ to 50℃, 10 to 80% relative humidity non-condensing, and 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition - Temperature: -10℃ to 50℃ - Humidity: 10 to 80% relative humidity (non-condensing) - Air Pressure: 500 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: General daily use is less than 9 hours per day, with reasonably foreseeable worst case continuous battery charging use for 24 hours.
evidence.evidenceText: General daily use : Less than 9 hours per day - Reasonably foreseeable worst case use scenarios : Assume continuous use for battery charging (using a day 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The ADD uses an indirect detector with a scintillator, phosphor, TFT array, and photodiodes to produce a digital image transferred to a PC.
evidence.evidenceText: Indirect type detectors are made up of a scintillator to convert x-rays to visible light and a phosphor to convert the light to electrons, which is read by a TFT array. ... The generated digital image data is transferred to a PC.

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include reading the user manual, connecting power/data/LAN/trigger/communication cables, powering on the detector, positioning the patient and detector, and calibrating the detector by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 Familiarize yourself with the user manual ... 8 Power on the detector 9 Arrange the patient in the correct position ... 10 Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: The device is used with an X-ray device, PACS, and MWL according to the usability safety characteristics checklist.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray device (Generator) Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Modality Worklist (MWL)

evidence.claim: The document identifies periodic calibration as necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD will need periodic calibration.

evidence.claim: The document states the device requires professional use.
evidence.evidenceText: Need professional use

evidence.claim: Information for safe use is provided by user manual and label.
evidence.evidenceText: Provided user manual and label

evidence.claim: The document identifies the ADD as a portable device.
evidence.evidenceText: Portable device

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable usability hazards include calibration operation with PC, electromagnetic environment influence, UI control error, indicator error, and battery use error.
evidence.evidenceText: UR-01 ... ADD can be operated with PC for calibration ... UR-02 ... electromagnetic wave ... UR-03 User control error ... UR-04 Indicator error ... UR-05 Battery use error

evidence.claim: Safety-related functions include detector and power supply power buttons and LED indicators.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector / Power supply - Power On/off button - LED indication

evidence.claim: Battery safety-related functions include battery remaining volume indicators for charging and not charging states.
evidence.evidenceText: Battery - Battery remaining volume - If the battery is not charging ... If the battery is charging

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable worst case scenarios include wiring disconnection, not attending to detector readiness before exposure, wrong wiring, dropping, excessive force, wrong calibration, high temperature, water or humidity, patient positioning by holding detector, mode selection, and network configuration.
evidence.evidenceText: During Normal Use - Wiring disconnection to Detector ... Use Errors - Wrong wiring ... Dropping ... Applying excessive force ... Wrong calibration ... Environment - High temperature ... Water/humidity ... Patient - Positioning by holding a Detector ... Reading and manipulation - Selection of mode ... Set up network configuration

evidence.claim: User interface requirements include user-friendly instructions, power-on instructions, operation checks before use, cleaning instructions, storage instructions, simple use method, inability to control output, and indicators.
evidence.evidenceText: Write user-friendly, easy-to-understand instructions ... Add the instructions how to turn on the device ... Add the indicator

evidence.claim: Recognizability requirements include manual font style and size, indicators, simple component configuration, and arranging controls and displays to facilitate user tasks.
evidence.evidenceText: Font style/font size of the user manual Indicator Simple component configuration Arrange controls and displays to facilitate user tasks

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to Usability Validation Plan (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039)

evidence.claim: The usability validation report is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040)

evidence.claim: The usability verification result records UI-01 through UI-07 as No issues and Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 No issues Pass UI-02 No issues Pass UI-03 No issues Pass UI-04 No issues Pass UI-05 No issues Pass UI-06 No issues Pass UI-07 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: The conclusion states risk control measures are sufficient and overall residual risk can be accepted.
evidence.evidenceText: risk control measures are sufficient ... overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal says FDA while the source path references CE MDD.
Potential conflicting sterilization evidence: E.2.1 states no parts are intended to be sterilized, while the hygienic context section states the device is intended to be sterilized by the user.
OCR/text extraction quality issues are present, including the Korean bookmark error text and obvious term-definition typos such as 'Person suing' and 'Men by which'.
Referenced validation plan A-TCF-5-039 and validation report A-TCF-5-040 are not included in this source text.
